project.name: Verity Release Evidence
project.description: A Verity demonstration: release claims and their evidence for a deliberately small storefront API.

claims.title: Order creation requires an authenticated customer
claims.whyItMatters: An unauthenticated order endpoint enables fraud, inventory abuse, and privacy failures.

evidence.description: The request boundary rejects callers before order data is created.
Executable tests exercise the anonymous, malformed, and authenticated paths.

claims.title: Public error responses never serialize server secrets
claims.whyItMatters: Agent-written error handling often leaks configuration or implementation details.

evidence.description: The public API uses a stable error code rather than returning thrown objects.
A narrow static guard catches a high-impact secret-exposure pattern.

claims.title: Every release claim is connected to reproducible evidence
claims.whyItMatters: A green test suite alone does not tell a reviewer which user promise the tests establish.

evidence.description: The configuration requires each claim to explain the user or business risk it protects.
A reviewer must confirm that the evidence still covers the intended release scope.
